title: Describe Image: promote the MPI-308 caption harness to a context-menu action on a real queued generation
description:
Promote the MPI-308 dev harness into a real feature. Right-click a gallery card or a history item -> 'Describe image' (video: 'Describe current frame'), run image_descriptor as a NORMAL QUEUED GENERATION (queue card + status-bar progress like any other job), and inject the resulting caption into the prompt box positive field.

WHY THE TRIGGER MOVES: the harness requires an image chip attached to the prompt box. Right-clicking the card you are looking at is the natural gesture and keeps the prompt box clean until the caption actually arrives. The caption STILL lands in the prompt box (same as the harness) — only the trigger changes.

WHAT ALREADY WORKS (MPI-308, shipped + user-verified in-app):
- comfy_workflows/image_descriptor.json — runtime workflow, synced from raw, abliterated Qwen3-VL 4B encoder (commit 0469039d). 18.1s warm.
- runImageDescribe() in js/services/commandExecutor.js — direct-engine runner, BYPASSES generationService.
- 'Describe Image' dev radial item + _describeFirstChip() in js/shell/navigation.js.
- Text capture is a GENERAL contract already (MPI-242): commandExecutor.js title-scoped Output_prompt scan + comfyOutputUrls.js readComfyOutputText.

THE ROOT BLOCKER — now unavoidable:
generationService.js `if (!urls.length)` in exec.onComplete treats a zero-media completion as a CANCELLED generation (emits tool:cancelled + generation:cancelled, ends the activity, fires callbacks.onCancel, returns) and discards promptText read one line earlier. image_descriptor returns TEXT ONLY, zero media. The harness dodged this by bypassing generationService entirely — but this card REQUIRES the queue + progress bar, so it must go THROUGH generationService and the gate must be fixed properly.

PER THE ROOT-CAUSE RULE: do NOT add `if (isCaptionOp) skip` at the gate. That is the symptom patch and will be rejected. The structural fix is an OP-LEVEL DECLARATION that a workflow returns text rather than media (e.g. `outputKind: 'text'` on the op in commandRegistry.js, defaulting to 'media' when absent), so completion branches on a declared contract instead of on an empty array. Keep the empty-array check for its real job: detecting genuinely empty completions and post-Stop terminals. Sweep every consumer of the new declaration in one pass.

RECON ALREADY DONE (2026-07-19, verified in source — re-verify anchors before relying on them, concurrent sessions edit these files):
- Context menus: MpiGalleryGrid.js (~line 1107 items array, ~1090 contextmenu listener) and MpiHistoryList.js (~line 229 items array, ~214 listener). SHARED renderer MpiContextMenu.show(), but the ITEMS ARRAYS ARE SEPARATE — the entry must be added TWICE.
- Item shape to copy: { key: 'compare', icon: 'compare', label: 'Compare', disabled: compareDisabled }
- onSelect receives ONLY `key` — never the item. Gallery reconstructs from _groups by id and emits to the parent block; History emits indices and MpiGroupHistoryBlock resolves _group.history[idx]. filePath is reached in the PARENT block, not in onSelect.
- Media-type conditionals already exist: MpiHistoryList uses `_isVideo` to push 'Combine' (video-only) and omit 'Download mask'. Copy that pattern for the video variant.
- Video current frame: captureFrameBlob() in js/utils/video.js returns { blob, dataUrl }; exposed as viewer.el.captureSnapshot() in MpiVideoViewer. NOTE frame-accuracy caveat — MPI-287 covers exact-last-frame capture via frameSink; for 'current frame' captureSnapshot is likely fine, but the blob must be written to a real path first because MpiLoadImageFromPath reads an OS PATH, not a blob (memory feedback_media_injection_path_to_string).
- Prompt box write: el.injectPrompts({ positive }) — direct call, or Events.emit('workspace:inject-prompts', { positive }). NOTE it always sets positiveValue but only repaints the textarea when NOT in negative mode.
- Chip/card path -> OS path: extractAbsPath() in js/utils/mediaActions.js.

WHAT NEEDS NO WORK (verified):
- StatusBar: needs NO changes. It shows a short label ('Describing image'), progress, and tool:idle — exactly like any other op. It CANNOT display the caption text itself (single short label element); the caption goes to the prompt box, not the status bar.
- progressStages: NO entry needed. A text-only workflow emits zero tqdm bars; stagesFor returns 0 and the counter simply omits the /M denominator.
- Queue panel: already degrades gracefully — _renderThumb falls through to an empty frame labelled 'TEXT' when there is no image/video, and previewUrl stays null with no latent previews.

STEPS:
1. Op-level text-output declaration + fix the generationService completion gate (THE REAL WORK). Sweep all consumers.
2. Register the op (commandRegistry.js, universal_workflows.js already has imageDescribe, operationRegistry.js, operation_registry.json — see docs/playbooks/add-app/01-descriptor-and-ops.md; NEVER regenerate operation_registry.json, it strips universal flags).
3. Context-menu entry in BOTH MpiGalleryGrid.js and MpiHistoryList.js; video variant gated on _isVideo.
4. Wire each parent block's handler: resolve filePath -> abs path -> enqueue via generationService -> on completion inject into the prompt box.
5. Video: capture current frame to a real file path before injection.
6. Retire or keep the dev radial harness (decide — it is dev-gated and harmless, but it duplicates the path).

WHY THIS IS WORTH BUILDING (user, 2026-07-19): the captioner is NOT upscale-only. Primary real workflow is describe -> EDIT THE TEXT -> generate: pull a reference image, caption it, adjust the description toward a target character, paint the face, LoRA-detail it, run the Head Swap app. The caption is a dense accurate STARTING POINT a human edits, which is why it must land in the prompt box (editable) rather than being stored and auto-consumed. That value is already demonstrated by the MPI-308 harness — it does NOT depend on the unproven caption->upscale claim. Upscale re-captioning remains a separate future consumer of the same op, gated on its own comparison.

STEP 0 — OWNERSHIP OF THE CAPTIONER WEIGHT (decide/build FIRST, user-decided 2026-07-19):
The abliterated encoder (qwen3vl_4b_abliterated_fp8_scaled.safetensors, 5.24GB) fits NEITHER existing owner. It is not a MODEL — nobody generates with it, and a ModelDef would force dead fields (workflows/qualityTiers/mediaType/gen_speed/ratio tables). It is not an APP — the trigger is a context-menu action, not an App Library tile. It is a CAPABILITY OTHER THINGS CALL, closer to a detector than to Krea2.

DECISION: introduce a THIRD entity — a PLUGIN — with its own registry alongside MODELS and APPS. Shape: { id, title, requiredDeps, operation } and nothing model-shaped. Rejected alternatives: a ModelDef with an isPlugin flag (carries dead fields, and EVERY model consumer must then honour the flag); hanging it off the Head Swap app via requiredDeps (wrong owner — the captioner is general, and gallery right-click would wrongly require that app installed); reusing the stock krea2-qwen3vl-clip (free, but the refusals return — user hit one 2026-07-19).

THREE THINGS THE PLUGIN ENTITY MUST SOLVE (all verified as real gaps):
1. OWNERSHIP/GATING — no model requires this weight, so nothing installs it today. It must install on demand when the feature is first used, NOT be forced on every user of some unrelated model.
2. GC PROTECTION (the dangerous one) — both uninstall guards in routes/downloadManager.js (_localSharedDepsMap and _remoteSharedDepIds) build their protected set from MODELS, unioned with _appRequiredDepIds(). A dep owned by NEITHER a model NOR an app is invisible to both, so ANY model uninstall would delete it. The plugin registry MUST be unioned into BOTH guards, exactly as apps are (see docs/playbooks/add-app/01-descriptor-and-ops.md § GC protection). Miss this and the feature silently breaks after an unrelated uninstall.
3. THE POD — do NOT bake 5.24GB into the Pod image for an optional captioner. Locally the user at least chose to install it; on the Pod they chose nothing. Fetch on first use / treat as an on-demand weight. Related: memory project_pod_weight_source_r2 (weights come from R2, not HF).

PRECEDENT TO FOLLOW: MPI-304 requiredDeps on apps solved the same class of problem (an app-only weight that would otherwise tax every user of a shared model). The plugin entity is that pattern generalised one step further. Dep entry still lives in the file for its KIND (assetDeps.js for this weight, beside krea2-qwen3vl-clip) — deps are filed by WHAT THEY ARE, never by who requires them.

SCOPE NOTE: storing the caption on the item (sidecar) and auto-feeding it to an upscale pass are DELIBERATELY OUT of this card. This card only moves the trigger and puts the job on the queue.

events.note: Split out of MPI-308: the dev harness is shipped and verified, this card promotes it to a context-menu action on a real queued generation. Forces the text-only completion contract that MPI-308 deliberately bypassed.

todo -> doing. STEP 0 built: PLUGIN entity (js/data/pluginsRegistry.js), assetDeps entry qwen3vl-abliterated-clip (sha256 45fe15d3...), GC protection unioned into BOTH uninstall guards in routes/downloadManager.js, tests/plugin-dep-gc.test.cjs passing incl. the ESM/CJS boundary. Weight uploading to R2. Gate fix decided: option (b), branch on outputKind early, above the empty-array check.

All 6 steps shipped. Step 0: PLUGIN entity + GC protection in BOTH guards, honouring the uninstall exclusion (unconditional protection would have made Uninstall a silent no-op). Weight uploaded to R2, HTTP 200, byte-exact. Steps 1-2: outputKind text declaration + gate branch ABOVE the empty-array check; op registered in commandRegistry + both operation registries. Consumer sweep: universal:true already gates it out of every picker. Steps 3-4: context-menu entry in BOTH items arrays; shared js/utils/describeAction.js. Step 6: dev harness retired. Bonus fix: injectPrompts silently swallowed a positive-only injection while in negative mode. Tests: plugin-dep-gc (mutation-checked), text-op-completion (28 ops in sync). Deferred: step 5 video current-frame capture (loader needs an OS path).

Krea2 encoder consolidation + identity-edit v1.2 (commits e2b0839e, 8e852aff, 9ad2df26, df056f54). All 4 Krea2 cards moved OFF krea2-qwen3vl-clip ONTO the abliterated weight this card introduced, making it a genuine shared dep (4 models + plugin). Stock encoder deleted from R2 and disk after verifying neither shipped tag (v0.0.12/v1.0.1) references it. Justified by a locked-seed A/B: 5/6 constraints honoured by BOTH encoders on an adversarial instruction-following prompt (the one miss, a negation, identical on both = Turbo/cfg-1.0 limit), and no sanitisation on a disinhibition probe. NOTE the switch bought disk+simplicity, NOT censorship relief — refusals came from the enhancer LLM downstream of Generate Text, never the CLIP. Identity LoRA v1.1->v1.2 (uploaded to R2, v1.1 deleted). User verified generation + edit in-app after reload. New cards MPI-312 (chip slot labels) / MPI-313 (chained multi-subject).

BUG FOUND — step 6 shared-dep uninstall FAILED destructively. Uninstalling the image-describer plugin DELETED qwen3vl_4b_abliterated_fp8_scaled.safetensors (5.24GB) even though four Krea2 cards declare it and Krea 2 Turbo NSFW was FULLY INSTALLED; that card dropped 100% -> 78% and is now broken. Primary hypothesis: stale require cache — routes/downloadManager.js uses createRequire (fully cached) and the server process predates this session's models.js edit, so the guard likely evaluated the OLD dep graph where no model required the abliterated weight. A renderer Ctrl+R does not restart the server. UNPROVEN — the restart test in the handoff decides it. Secondary: the fullyInstalled gate at _localSharedDepsMap:171 (probably not this incident, user confirms the card was fully installed, but a real latent hole). Unit test passed because it covers plugin-deps-during-model-uninstall, the OPPOSITE direction. Handoff: handoff-shared-dep-uninstall-bug.md

ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED. The handoff's primary hypothesis (stale require cache) was only the PROXIMATE cause; app.log shows TWO deletions (09:46:34 and 16:37:26), both logging 'kept 0 shared'. Server booted 09:39, models.js edited 12:21 (commit 8e852aff 13:02) — so the cache WAS stale at the second delete. But the restart test would have MASKED the real defect: the fullyInstalled gate (the handoff's dismissed secondary hypothesis) is CIRCULAR. A shared COMMON dep is itself an input to deriveInstalledOps' commonComplete, so once the weight went missing every model needing it stopped defending it — the damage was unrecoverable by design, and it cascades across a whole model family (the MPI-258 tier-cycle hole generalised). FIX: protection now keys on DECLARATION + any on-disk footprint, not completeness; installedOps still narrows which ops' deps are protected, and a damaged model falls back to its full universe. Applied to BOTH twins (_localSharedDepsMap + _remoteSharedDepIds) in one pass per the engine-split rule. TEST: tests/shared-dep-uninstall-direction.test.cjs covers the never-run direction (model deps during a PLUGIN uninstall); mutation-verified — case 2 fails against the old gate, passes against the fix. Also removed the dead krea2-qwen3vl-clip dep entry (zero consumers, R2 object deleted so its url 404s) + 2 stale comments. docs/download-manager.md updated with the circularity + the 'dep-graph edits need a SERVER restart' operational trap. NOT YET DONE: user must re-download the 5.24GB weight to unbreak Krea 2 Turbo NSFW, and the fix is logic+unit verified only — a live uninstall on a restarted server is unverified.

FIX v2 — the first fix (dd3f97fa, any-footprint) REINTRODUCED MPI-258 B1. User asked whether MPI-276 was checked: it had not been, and its research/04-bug-history-invariants.md holds invariant 5 (whole-model-installed sharers) for exactly the B1 reason — the pre-258 per-dep rule was circular the OPPOSITE way, stranding ~19GB of LTX tier-family deps as undeletable. Proved empirically: with any-footprint, uninstalling ltx-23 with both transformers absent stranded 11/11 shared deps. REAL RULE: install evidence must come from a model's EXCLUSIVE deps (files no other model declares) — exclusive deps can never be someone else's footprint (kills B1) and can never be the shared file under judgement (kills 310). CRITICAL SCOPING TRAP found live: exclusivity computed against the guard's `others` list is WRONG (it omits the uninstall target, making its shared deps look exclusive to the sibling — B1 again); _multiModelDepIds() computes it over the whole registry. Both twins updated. Test now pins BOTH circularities (mutation-verified both directions: old gate fails case 2, others-scoped exclusivity fails case 4). Plus the UX gap user flagged: a plugin uninstall that frees nothing now toasts 'Nothing was freed — still needed by <models>' (keptShared[].sharedWith surfaced); plugin row stays Installed because pluginAvailability reads disk truth. docs/download-manager.md rewritten with the two-circularities table + pointer to the MPI-276 invariants dossier.

Toast/notification pass (f2ace407, 0f9d8910). User challenged my claim that there was 'no install-completion toast by design' — I had grepped only 2 files and was WRONG. A Downloads desktop-notification toggle exists (MpiSettings, notificationPrefs.downloads) and notificationService DOES notify on download:complete. Two real defects found: (1) DUPLICATE TOAST — MpiModelManager already had a full uninstall toast ladder incl. a 'Nothing to remove' branch, so the toast I added in downloadService (48b76ace) was a second surface; reverted, improved the existing one instead to NAME the holders from keptShared[].sharedWith. (2) UNFOCUSED COMPLETION LOST — the download:complete handler returned after sending the OS notification, so nothing surfaced in-app; a missed OS notification (focus assist/fullscreen/dismissed) left zero trace after a multi-GB install, exactly the walk-away flow the user described. Fixed: unfocused completions queue a toast flushed on the next window `focus` (not visibilitychange — an Electron window can be visible but unfocused). User chose this option over both-immediately / persistent activity list. (3) Same plugin-name bug in BOTH surfaces: modelId is `plugin:<id>`, absent from MODELS, so the raw key leaked into toast + notification text; both now resolve PLUGINS titles. User's design point recorded: install completion deserves notification MORE than uninstall (async + walk-away vs synchronous button press). STILL UNVERIFIED: the live plugin-uninstall test.

attention.reason: DESTRUCTIVE BUG, USER DATA LOST. Plugin uninstall deleted a 5.24GB weight that four Krea2 cards depend on; Krea 2 Turbo NSFW is broken (100% -> 78%) and the encoder must be re-downloaded before Krea2 generates at all. READ handoff-shared-dep-uninstall-bug.md AND docs/download-manager.md before touching routes/downloadManager.js — partial card bars are COMPLETENESS, not download progress, and misreading them sends you down the wrong root cause. First action is the restart test that decides between stale-require-cache and a genuinely broken guard. Fix both engine twins in one pass.
